name: Mughal Empire
note: Babur was Timurid (Turkic-Mongol); court was thoroughly Persianized. Akbar's syncretic 'Din-i-Ilahi' experiment attempted to bridge Hindu and Muslim identities — shows religion as a live political variable even within a single regime

figures.name: Babur
figures.significance: Timurid prince who conquered India; his Baburnama is one of the great autobiographies

figures.name: Akbar the Great
figures.significance: Consolidated the empire; policy of religious tolerance; abolished jizya

figures.name: Shah Jahan
figures.significance: Built the Taj Mahal; apex of Mughal Persian-Indian cultural synthesis

figures.name: Aurangzeb
figures.significance: Largest territorial extent; Sharia policies alienated Hindus; his death began the collapse
